skill.name: when-configuring-sandbox-security-use-sandbox-configurator
skill.description: Configure Claude Code sandbox security using documented file isolation, network restrictions, resource limits, tests, and monitoring steps.
skill.summary: Guide Claude Code sandbox hardening with file, network, testing, and monitoring controls.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell, filesystem, network, and sensitive-file patterns. Review found these are primarily documentation and generated-script examples for sandbox hardening, not confirmed malicious behavior, but they include privileged host changes and negative tests against sensitive paths that require clear user review before use.

medium_findings.title: Privileged Host Security Configuration Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to create scripts that write to system locations, load AppArmor profiles, and change iptables defaults. This is legitimate sandbox-hardening content, but mistakes could disrupt host access or weaken controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly show writes under /etc and firewall policy changes. The context is defensive configuration, so the risk is operational rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive File Access Used as Negative Security Tests
medium_findings.description: The generated test suite attempts to read /etc/shadow and ~/.ssh/id_rsa to verify access is blocked. The intent is defensive validation, but these probes touch credential-sensitive paths and should only run in an isolated test environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly references sensitive files, but the surrounding conditions expect failure and mark successful reads as security issues. This supports a true sensitive-access risk with defensive intent.

medium_findings.title: Network and Packet Monitoring Commands Require Elevated Review
medium_findings.description: The skill includes outbound network allow rules, domain blocking, curl-based connectivity checks, and tcpdump monitoring. These commands are relevant to sandbox validation but can expose traffic metadata or alter host network behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit and security-focused. No evidence of exfiltration was found, but the commands have enough host impact to require caution.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Documentation Blocks
low_findings.description: Many static findings are fenced markdown examples, ASCII diagrams, memory-key strings, or placeholder blocked domains. They do not execute by themselves and do not show credential exfiltration or malicious command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation, graph structure, or blocked-domain placeholders. They explain or test controls rather than directing covert network activity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Scripts with Root-Level System Changes
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill creates shell scripts that modify /etc/security, /etc/apparmor.d, /etc/hosts, and firewall policies. Users should review commands and run them only in intended sandbox hosts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts and privileged paths are directly visible. Their purpose is hardening, but the host-level effects are substantial.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential-Sensitive Path Probing
dangerous_patterns.description: Security tests probe /etc/shadow and ~/.ssh/id_rsa to confirm access is denied. This should never be adapted to collect, print, or transmit file contents.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sensitive paths are directly referenced in the test script. The defensive assertion is clear, but the pattern remains sensitive.

content.value_statement: Sandbox setup can fail when file and network boundaries are unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through policies, scripts, tests, and monitoring.
actual_capabilities: Documents sandbox security requirements and threat models.
Defines file access policies with allowed, denied, and read-only paths.
Drafts shell scripts for file isolation, network isolation, testing, and monitoring.
Creates validation tests for blocked sensitive paths and allowed workspace paths.
Records deployment guidance and security summary documentation.
limitations: Requires careful review before running privileged host commands.
Uses example policies that may not match every operating system or sandbox runtime.
Does not prove compliance without independent security review.
Network allow lists must be customized for each environment.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Secure AI Coding Workspace
use_cases.description: Define file boundaries, trusted domains, and audit requirements before enabling AI coding workflows.

use_cases.title: Validate Sandbox Controls in CI
use_cases.description: Generate repeatable tests that check blocked sensitive paths, allowed workspace writes, and network restrictions.

use_cases.title: Document Operational Sandbox Controls
use_cases.description: Create deployment notes, monitoring steps, and maintenance checklists for sandbox governance.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Sandbox Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to assess my Claude Code sandbox requirements. Focus on file boundaries, network access, and resource limits.

prompt_templates.title: Draft File Isolation Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a file isolation policy for a workspace-only sandbox. Include denied sensitive paths and test criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Build Validation Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design sandbox security tests for blocked credential paths, allowed workspace writes, and trusted network access.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Hardening Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my production sandbox hardening plan. Identify risky commands, missing rollback steps, and monitoring gaps.

output_examples.input: Assess sandbox requirements for a team using Claude Code on shared build hosts.
output_examples.output: A threat model, access policy outline, trusted domain list, and validation checklist for shared-host sandbox use.

output_examples.input: Create tests for file and network isolation before release.
output_examples.output: A test plan that checks sensitive-path denial, workspace write access, trusted endpoint reachability, and blocked-domain behavior.

output_examples.input: Prepare deployment documentation for sandbox monitoring.
output_examples.output: A deployment guide with prechecks, rollback notes, monitoring targets, and maintenance review intervals.

best_practices: Review every generated shell command before execution on a host.
Run validation first in a disposable sandbox or staging environment.
Customize allowed domains, ports, and paths for the actual workload.
anti_patterns: Running privileged firewall or system-file changes on a personal workstation without review.
Copying example allow lists into production without confirming business needs.
Changing sensitive-file tests to print or store secret file contents.

faq.question: Does this skill execute sandbox changes by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides documented commands and scripts that a user must review and run.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for production systems?
faq.answer: Use it only after review. Some examples change host firewall and security configuration.

faq.question: Why does the audit show medium risk?
faq.answer: The skill includes privileged commands and sensitive-path tests, but no confirmed malicious intent was found.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code support.

faq.question: Does it replace a formal security review?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure sandbox controls, but compliance and production approval need independent review.

faq.question: What should I customize first?
faq.answer: Start with allowed paths, denied paths, trusted domains, open ports, and rollback procedures.
